params.text:
Active participation

Active participation means recognising the person as an active partner in their own care and daily life, rather than a passive recipient. It is about doing things with people, not to or for them.

Even small steps count. Someone might not be able to prepare a whole meal, but they can choose the ingredients, stir the pot, or lay the table. Active participation:

  • Maintains and builds skills and independence.
  • Increases self-esteem, purpose and a sense of belonging.
  • Reduces isolation and the risk of learned helplessness.

Look for what a person can do and enable it, offering support only where it is needed.

params.question: Active participation means doing tasks for the person as quickly as possible so they do not have to make any effort.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Not quite. Doing everything for someone removes their independence. Active participation enables the person to take part as much as they can.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. Active participation is about doing things WITH people and enabling them to take part — not doing everything for them.
